intended audience: Intended for all! The impact of community extends to every discipline, from Product to Marketing and everything in between.

session title: Crafting an Ever-Expanding Jianghu: Open-World Design and Sustainable Update Pipelines in 'Where Winds Meet' (Presented by Everstone Studio)
description:
Where Winds Meet aims to create engaging open-world play in a wuxia setting, a Chinese martial arts fantasy genre. While building free, immersive exploration, the game blends a romantic sensibility with a grounded sense of historical realism, shaping its audiovisual direction and overall player experience. As an open-world wuxia game, Where Winds Meet features several distinctive design approaches. Having both solo and multiplayer modes enables a flexible and uncommon gameplay experience. Meanwhile, such a long-term live operation model with frequent updates places huge demands on both content design and the production pipeline.

Join Lead Producer, Beralt Lyu, for a deep dive into how the Where Winds Meet team tackles the challenges of crafting wuxia immersion while integrating solo and multiplayer experiences. Discover how the team builds a production pipeline to support long-term live operations.
takeaway: Attendees will gain insight into content design processes for crafting wuxia immersion, as well as strategies for supporting rapid updates within a long-term live operations model.
intended audience: This talk is for anyone who interested in tackling immersive Wuxia open world development and sustaining rapid content updates through live operations. It does not cover technical implementation.

session title: Insight to Action: Innovating Ops with AI featuring Valorem Reply (Presented by Microsoft Xbox)
description: Launch week is the moment a game studio discovers whether it can ship faster than chaos can compound. Join Microsoft and Valorem Reply as we demonstrate how studios can leverage AI to shorten the distance between insight and action—while keeping creators firmly in control. Attendees see how Valorem Reply's Agentic Gaming Intelligence Foundry, working with a Microsoft Fabric Data Agent, transforms live telemetry, player sentiment, and operational data into faster fixes, smarter decisions, and calmer launches. Through real-world scenarios, the session shows how AI accelerates crash resolution, surfaces unexpected player behavior insights, and absorbs launch-day support spikes without removing humans from the loop. The focus remains on empowering teams, not replacing them. The result is a studio that launches with confidence, uncovers new directions and opportunities and stays focused on what matters most: creating great games.
takeaway: Attendees will see live demos of Organizational AI in action—detecting and fixing crashes faster, turning studio data into testable insights, and scaling launch‑day support without adding headcount—showing how game studios become more responsive, resilient, and scalable while keeping humans firmly in control and walk away with the guidance on how they can set up their own intuitive systems using Microsoft AI foundations with the support of Valorem Reply.
intended audience: Target audience for this session is studio leaders, producers, live‑ops, engineering, data, and support teams responsible for launch readiness and live operations or anyone who is curious on how AI can be practically applied with real studio value and impact to streamline ops and reduce crunch.
speakers: Uthappa Kattera Chengappa(Microsoft), Mithuna Srinivasan(Valorem Reply), Rob Nelson

session title: Let the Engine Understand You: Intent-driven Game Scene Editor Powered by AI (Presented by Tencent Games AI)
description: This talk presents a large-scale world-generation system that relies on natural interactions such as voice commands. Users could generates physically plausible, visually realistic scenes; through natural speech enabled by LLM and MCP, they iteratively refine and optimize the results. No professional artistic or technical skills are required—anyone could craft production-quality worlds.
takeaway: The audience will see a practice that dramatically lowers the entry barrier and boosts the output quality for non-artists. Ordinary players effortlessly craft believable scenes. Unlocking fresh, inventive gameplay possibilities.
intended audience: Attendees who track procedural-generation technology in games, anyone who follows UGC ecosystems, and anyone who monitors LLM applications in game engines will all leave with concrete takeaways.No specific prior knowledge is required, yet familiarity with MCP or UE PCG lets them takeaway more.
speakers: Zheng Jiang(Tencent Games), Dong Ye(Tencent Games), Yingpeng Zhang(Tencent Games)

session title: Level Up Your Game: Build Smarter, Scale Faster with Alibaba Cloud & Qwen AI (Presented by Alibaba Cloud)
description:
AI is already transforming games—but most studios are stuck on chatbots and concept art.
At Alibaba Cloud, we help developers embed real intelligence across their entire pipeline: dynamic NPCs with Qwen, auto-generated worlds with Wan, AI-driven QA, and live-ops powered by real-time player analytics—all running on a global cloud infrastructure built for gaming.
In this session, you'll see real, production-ready examples from studios using:
● Qwen-Agents to generate quests, dialogue, and terrain
● AI that tests like a human—but never sleeps
● Predictive systems that boost retention and reduce churn
Plus, learn how our global network (US, EU, APAC, MEA) delivers sub-50ms latency, auto-scales at launch, and cuts cloud costs by up to 40%.
Leave with practical ways to add AI to your existing stack—and scale globally without compromise.
takeaway:
● Discover what's possible today: See demos of Qwen-Max and Qwen-VL generating game dialogue, quests, and assisting in asset creation—with Wan enabling multimodal reasoning.
● Integrate fast, not from scratch: Use ready-to-go samples and APIs to bring AI into Unity/Unreal workflows—no model training needed.
● Scale globally with gaming-grade infrastructure: Deploy on Alibaba Cloud's low-latency network (US, EU, APAC) with dedicated game servers, edge acceleration, and real-time monitoring.
● Stay flexible: Run open models (Llama, Mistral) on our CUDA-compatible GPU instances—or use Qwen via API. Your stack, your rules.
intended audience:
Game developers, technical directors, studio CTOs, and innovation leads who want to:
● Reduce content creation time and operational costs
● Enhance player engagement through intelligent systems
● Launch and scale online games across global markets with confidence
speakers: Alex Chen(Alibaba Cloud), Jeff Fu(Alibaba Cloud)

session title: Neural Graphics in Practice for Next-Gen Game Development (Presented by Arm)
description:
**Part 1 – Sergio Alapont (Arm)**

In this talk, we present the rendering pipeline behind Sumo Digital's new mobile case study featuring fully ray-traced lighting. We explain how we are pushing real-time performance on mobile hardware by combining aggressive pipeline engineering with Arm's neural rendering technologies, including neural super-resolution, neural denoising, and neural frame-rate upscaling.



**Part 2 – Willen Yang (Arm)**

Game development is entering a new era where AI-driven rendering and neural techniques redefine performance and visual fidelity. Arm's Neural Graphics SDK (NG SDK) brings advanced neural-based rendering, denoising, and super-resolution capabilities to game engines, enabling developers to achieve desktop-quality graphics on mobile and edge devices without sacrificing frame rates.
takeaway:
**Part 1 – Sergio Alapont (Arm)**

The session covers how the frame is structured, how we budget GPU time to sustain 60 Hz, and how we validate performance in the absence of final hardware. We'll also dig into practical lessons learned while bringing ray-traced lighting and neural graphics to a mobile production environment—what worked, what didn't, and where the real bottlenecks were.

**Part 2 – Willen Yang (Arm)**

This session introduces Arm's neural graphics SDK architecture, Unreal Engine plugins built on the NG SDK, and the IQ improvements enabled by real content and practical workflows. Learn how to leverage neural graphics to optimize rendering pipelines, reduce GPU load, and deliver stunning visuals across diverse platforms. We showcase real-world examples, performance benchmarks, and best practices for deploying neural rendering in production titles.

session title: Next-Generation of In-Game AI Experiences: Snapdragon Game AI SDK (Presented by Qualcomm Technologies, Inc.)
description: This session introduces the Snapdragon Game AI SDK, a new toolkit that makes it easy for developers to bring powerful AI into their games for local inference. We'll showcase tools that enable richer, more dynamic gameplay, including Automatic Speech Recognition, Large Language Models, and Text‑to‑Speech for AI‑driven NPC interactions. Learn how to integrate these capabilities seamlessly to unlock the power efficiency and NPU performance of Snapdragon platforms.
takeaway: Attendees will leave with a clear understanding of how to integrate new AI‑driven experiences into their games using the turnkey solutions provided in the Game AI SDK. You'll learn practical, developer‑friendly approaches for adding these capabilities into your existing workflow and bringing on‑device AI features to life with ease.
intended audience: Game Developers, Engineers, Technical Designers, and Gameplay programmers interested in integrating scalable AI powered systems that enhance player experiences.

session title: The Future of Fair Play: What AI Means for Multiplayer Games Over the Next Five Years (Presented by Community Clubhouse)
description:
The games industry is entering a period of rapid change, and AI is reshaping both sides of the fight against cheating. Cheat developers are already using AI to mimic human behavior, mask suspicious patterns, and automate entire cheat operations at scale. In response, studios and security partners are deploying AI-driven systems to detect non-human and unfair play faster, earlier, and with greater precision.

This session explores how AI is redefining the battle for fair play, what the next generation of cheating threats is likely to look like, and how studios can stay ahead of them without resorting to intrusive systems that undermine player trust or experience.
takeaway:
- How AI-driven cheating makes detecting cheats harder and poses a real threat to multiplayer games
- The pressure this puts publishers, particularly smaller ones, under to keep up
- The critical balance between protection and player privacy
- New expectations from regulators and platform holders
intended audience: The intended audience for this talk is game industry professionals who are currently in a leadership or decision making role in game development or interested in learning more about how AI can both help protect the player experience, as well as potentially harm it.
speakers: Andrew Hogan(Intorqa), Phillip Koskinas(Riot Games), Kyle Boerstler(Activision)

session title: The Power of Partnership: How EA SPORTS and Amazon Are Elevating the Sports Fan Experience with Madden NFL 26 (Presented by Amazon Web Services)
description: Discover how EA SPORTS and Amazon joined forces to launch a 360° Madden NFL 26 activation—an ambitious effort to elevate and expand how fans engage with sports. By combining EA SPORTS' expertise in game development and immersive digital engagement with Amazon's expansive technology and media platforms, the 2025 partnership brought multi-faceted experiences to sports fans and gamers across Prime Video, Luna, Twitch, Amazon Ads, Amazon.com and more. This collaboration represents a strong first step, showcasing what's possible when two industry leaders unite their strengths across streaming, digital engagement, and retail. The session will highlight key outcomes from this inaugural year, real-world learnings, and the emerging trends that make EA SPORTS and Amazon ideally positioned to shape the future of fandom by giving fans a richer, interactive experience.
takeaway: Attendees will learn how to leverage EA SPORTS and Amazon's full portfolio to create integrated player experiences that drive measurable business outcomes. You'll gain practical frameworks for coordinating multi-platform activations and understand how to position comprehensive partnership proposals that demonstrate unique value beyond single-service offerings.
intended audience: This session targets game publishers, sports franchise executives, and business development leaders seeking to maximize partnership value through integrated platform strategies. Marketing and product leaders interested in cross-platform player engagement will also benefit. No technical prerequisites required—focus is on strategic partnership development and execution.
speakers: Ashley Holinger(AWS), Trevor Moore(AWS), Aimee Munton(AWS), Jesse Sell(Twitch), Andrew Echanique(Electronic Arts (EA)), Ryan Bodlaender(Electronic Arts (EA))

session title: VR Performance Fundamentals for Quest 3/3S (Presented by Meta)
description: Practical deep dive into hitting and holding frame-rate on Meta Quest 3/3S. Learn why performance matters more in VR than any other medium, how to diagnose bottlenecks, and how to use the latest profiling and optimization tools to ship smoother, more comfortable experiences.

session title: AI Trends of Today and Opportunities for Tomorrow: Ask Me Anything
description:
Bubble or not, machine learning is here, and it works. Advanced techniques in Machine Learning have already been widely applied in the video game industry, powering products that are available on the market today, such as Nvidia DLSS and Bandai Namco Tekken8 'Super Ghost Battle' system. From content creation to runtime optimization and the underlying fabric of live service features, Machine Learning is increasingly becoming the solution to the problems many developers face. In fact, it has the potential to the whole video games industry by storm in the coming years, in many disruptive ways.

For leaders and investors, it's essential to understand where we are, and where this technology is going. Esteemed industry leader Julien Merceron will share not only his own insights and opinions on the trends and futures of Machine Learning, but the voices of many other industry experts.

This session is designed to prioritize YOUR questions, offering a unique opportunity to explore trends, challenges, and opportunities in an open forum. Join us for an hour of discussion, this session is all yours! Ask anything!
takeaway: While the moderator will have questions prepared for the panelists, the focus will be on audience questions and participation. This forum will give attendees insights into the hot questions of the moment, the current and future trends in Machine Learning, and the opportunity to have their own questions addressed. Attendees will leave this session with new connections in the world of Machine Learning AI, as well as concrete, actionable information to advance their initiatives and refine their company's strategy.
intended audience: This panel is designed for CXOs, investors, leaders in the video games industry, and anyone interested in learning more about how Machine Learning and AI will shape the future of the video game industry. With a focus on addressing all audience questions, this forum is designed to be accessible to all, with any level of familiarity with Machine Learning and its applications.
location: Blue Shield of California Theater, YBCA
speakers: Bryan Catanzaro(NVIDIA), Julien Merceron(Orion Productions), Alexandre Moufarek(Google DeepMind), Zhen Zhai(Blizzard Entertainment), Ross O'Dwyer(Meta)

session title: 15 More Games in 15 More Years
description:
At GDC 2011, Stone spoke about 15 tabletop games he created for his family and close friends. 15 years later, he has continued this tradition of designing a new game every year: a play-by-mail game for his sons in college, a real-time rhythm card game for drum students, a social game for a wedding, and—most recently—several highly personalized tabletop games for his four grandchildren.
In this session, Stone details those 15 games and describes how his design philosophy evolved during that time. Each game teaches its own special lesson, with unique constraints shaped by the experiences of his small audience. Many of the tips and techniques can be applied to games that you create for the people closest in your life.
takeaway: You do not need a large team, money, or the latest hardware and software to create games. All you need is inspiration, free time, and a few close friends. By crafting small personal games, free from the compromises typically associated with the game industry, you will grow as a designer.
intended audience: This talk is for all game designers interested in exploring the art and craft of creating games without the financial and time constraints imposed by corporate oversight. It will especially interest parents of young children, and anyone who has ever wanted to create a personal tabletop game for their closest friends.

session title: Back to Basics: Translating Your Invisible Marketing Foundation into Visibility
description: Many devs love to skip all the behind-the-scenes marketing prep work that no one publicly sees. Traditional marketing best practices can seem obtuse or even useless when you do not understand the benefit they provide to marketing a game. In this talk, we will demonstrate why it's crucial to not skip this behind-the-scenes work and how things like SWOT analysis, market research, audience research, and more translate into actual marketing assets, more effective trailers, and impactful strategies. We will provide real-life examples of marketing assets and decisions created as a result of these foundational marketing pillars leaving devs with an understanding of why they matter, how to conduct them, and the benefits for their game.
takeaway: Why it's crucial to not skip the behind-the-scenes marketing before announcing your game and how traditional marketing processes like market research and SWOT analysis practically translate into strategies and tangible marketing assets with greater impact.
intended audience: Game marketers who understand best practices but lack experience creating actual marketing assets. Developers who are unsure about the benefits of working with marketers.
speakers: Dana Trebella(Spoke and Wheel Strategy), Derek Lieu(Derek Lieu Creative)

session title: Balancing Work for Hire Roundtable: Original IP Development
description: Many studios, new and old, are using a version of work-for-hire as a way to keep their teams afloat while developing original IP. While common, this model brings a unique set of challenges - like managing team bandwidth across projects, maintaining internal momentum, and navigating unpredictable client timelines alongside your own projects. In this roundtable, studio leaders currently balancing client work with their own original game development come together to share strategies, pitfalls, and what has worked for them. Moderated by the CEO of Petricore, Ryan Canuel, who has led a hybrid studio for over a decade - the session encourages open discussion around operations, staffing, sales, and sustainability. Attendees will leave with real-world insights and tactics they can bring back to their own teams to better manage the work-for-hire model while still moving original projects forward.
takeaway: Attendees gain insight into how other studios structure and manage work-for-hire alongside original development. By sharing their own experiences and hearing a wide range of real-world approaches, studio leaders walk away with new perspectives and practical ideas for tackling the challenges of running a hybrid studio.
intended audience: This talk is primarily indented for business leaders within game studios that are actively doing a hybrid mix of work-for-hire alongside original IP development. Come prepared to share lessons from your own studios, and hear from others on similar topics.

session title: Beyond the Console Roundtable: Building Authenticity and Inclusive Spaces for AAPI Voices Everywhere (Presented by the IGDA)
description: Community doesn't end at the controller. Hosted by AAPI In Gaming, this roundtable is a space to explore how we create and nurture belonging beyond the bounds of the gaming industry. From food and mental health to creative expression and representation, we'll discuss how our diverse voices are shaping a more connected world. Join us as we allow attendees to share personal stories, community-building strategies, and visions for an inclusive future where AAPI identity and creativity can thrive in every space we play, create, and exist.
takeaway:
'Our community, our spaces. Built by us, for us, beyond the game.'
Whether you are currently in the industry or have moved on to new chapters, you belong here. This session focuses on the whole person, not just the job title. We will equip attendees with the knowledge to craft authentic AAPI narratives and the courage to challenge representation harms. We believe that gaming, culture, creativity, and wellness are deeply connected. Come discover how your whole self—not just your gamer identity—can be the bridge to meaningful community.
intended audience: This conversation is for the community builders, the creative minds, and the passionate advocates who believe our connections should thrive everywhere. While hosted by AAPI In Gaming, this roundtable is intentionally designed to bridge worlds. We invite AAPI professionals and enthusiasts from the gaming industry to connect with creators, leaders, and organizers from all fields—from food and wellness to art and tech. If you are an ally dedicated to fostering inclusive spaces or a student looking to build a network with purpose, your voice is essential to this discussion.
speakers: Mika Dilig(AAPI in Gaming + Gay Gaming Professionals), Abbie Hamilton(AAPI In Gaming)

session title: 'Civilization VII': Impactful UI Sound Design at Scale
description: UI sound design is not only necessary, it is an opportunity to bring your game to the next level. But how to ensure it is communicating clearly and intuitively, staying on brand, works well in repetition, and is also fun? In this presentation, Assoc. Audio Director Kadet Kuhne offers a deep dive into the strategies and processes behind designing UI sounds at scale for Civilization VII. The talk explores how a UI sound design system was built efficiently and effectively, incorporating hierarchies and key design principles to support Civilization VII's new visual style. With that foundation in place, building out polished UI with bespoke, engaging sounds will be fully detailed with real-world examples of challenges, solutions, and impactful results. Additionally, the presentation covers implementation within dynamic and complex game systems to ensure UI audio remains communicative, fun, and fully optimized throughout gameplay.
takeaway: Attendees will gain insight into how UI sound design can enrich the player's connection with a game, creating compelling interactive experiences. Whether you are a sound designer, developer, or UI/UX specialist, this presentation will provide valuable strategies for incorporating meaningful, on-brand, and enjoyable audio in UI.
intended audience: This talk is for anyone interested in creating UI sounds or setting up UI sound design systems, UI/UX enthusiasts, game developers, and sound designers who want to dive deeper into crafting impactful, communicative SFX for games.

session title: Cultivating UX Community: UX Maturity & Resilience Networking Session
description:
The current state of the industry has been fraught with the loss of knowledge, expertise, and loss of momentum. How do we pick up the pieces to cultivate UX maturity and innovation? How do we maintain creativity and design enthusiasm when our professional future has been uncertain for years. Does the answer lie in community and grassroots knowledge sharing?

Join this networking session to create meaningful connections through open and vulnerable conversations around our hopes for the future of UX maturity. As well, sharing our resilience strategies that have been guiding you through these tumultuous times

session title: Designing Stadium: Crafting a New Game Mode for 'Overwatch'
description:
Join Blizzard designers Dylan and Scott as they break down the 18-month journey of creating Stadium, a brand-new game mode for Overwatch. From initial concept and design goals to prototyping, balancing, and post-launch support, this talk offers a deep dive into the challenges of building innovative experiences within an established franchise.

Learn how the team iterated on gameplay systems, expanded hero functionality, and integrated player feedback through live testing events and limited betas. The session also explores how Stadium maintained the core Overwatch feel while introducing new mechanics, camera systems, and strategic depth.

Attendees will leave with practical insights into cross-discipline collaboration, iterative design, and how to successfully launch new game modes in competitive multiplayer environments.
takeaway:
• Systemic Innovation in a Live Game: Learn how to introduce bold new mechanics—like a shop system or 3rd-person camera—without breaking the identity of a well-established competitive shooter.
• Hero Design in a New Context: Understand how to adapt and expand hero abilities to fit a new mode while preserving their core fantasy and balance integrity.
• Balancing Through Data and Playtesting: See how internal dashboards, live event data, and focused feedback loops were used to guide tuning and ensure clarity, competitiveness, and replayability.
• Prototyping for Clarity and Fun: Discover practical lessons from early failed prototypes, including what made them fall short and how those failures informed later success.
• Collaborative Iteration: Gain insight into cross-functional workflows—from design and animation to VFX and UI—and how alignment was maintained across disciplines during rapid iteration.
• Live Testing and Community Feedback: Learn how limited-time events and betas can be leveraged as safe spaces for experimentation and trust-building with your player base.
intended audience:
• Game Designers working on live games or multiplayer systems who are exploring how to innovate within existing frameworks.
• Systems and Combat Designers interested in hero design, balance, and integrating new mechanics into established character kits.
• UX/VFX/UI Designers focused on visual clarity and feedback in fast-paced competitive environments.
• Producers and Team Leads looking for insights on cross-discipline collaboration, iteration pipelines, and live testing workflows.
• Developers at Large Studios navigating the challenges of shipping new modes inside beloved franchises with vocal communities.

session title: Diversity in Game Audio Roundtable Day 1
description:
In a time of political uncertainty, industry layoffs, and risk-averse decision-making, open conversations about diversity and inclusion are more urgent than ever. This multi-day roundtable invites participants to explore bold topics like surviving and resisting political tension, self-advocacy, and allyship. Through deep, focused dialogue led by a variety of voices, we will examine how to support marginalized communities and drive change wherever you are in the industry. Join us to move beyond performative talk and toward actionable steps for a more inclusive future.

Day 1: Fighting Back: How to take care of yourself as a marginalized person & offer your support as a person of privilege

Day 2: Making it Happen and Advocating for Yourself: How to use your privilege to make change & where/how to find support as a marginalized person
takeaway: Attendees will participate in a deeper discussion of challenges facing diverse game audio professionals under current political climates and further enhance the cognizance of unconscious biases. They will unearth practical tools for creating safe spaces for diversity, promoting properly with hiring and team-building, creating genuine inclusive work environments, and driving industry-wide change.
intended audience: This roundtable is for all game audio professionals, from entry-level to veterans, including those from underrepresented groups and allies. It is relevant for individual contributors, team leads, hiring managers, and decision-makers interested in inclusivity. Anyone involved in game audio or shaping the industry's culture will find valuable insights for promoting diversity.

session title: IGDA Mentor Cafe Session 1
description:
The IGDA considers mentorship to be one of the critical cornerstones on which we must build a strong and diverse industry. As such, the IGDA Mentor Café program provides a way for developers to support each other and connect in person through mentorship. This event is open to GDC attendees, but individuals must register in advance as a Mentor or Mentee. Space is limited.

Register here!﻿ (https://igda.peoplegrove.com/hub/igda/programs/igda-mentor-cafe-gdc/about)
speakers: No speakers found for this session

session title: K12 and Youth Roundtable Day 1: Education Community
description:
Teaching Game Development in High Schools, Middle Schools, and Youth Activity camp settings presents unique challenges that are distinct from how post-secondary education approaches Game Development, while also differing from how other secondary educators approach the instruction of other disciplines. These issues are compounded by a lack of communication between educators, often resulting in educators having to teach in isolation, with limited meaningful support available at the site, district, county, or even state level.

This roundtable aims to identify common challenges associated with proposing, creating, and teaching Game Development in a Youth Educational setting. Allowing educators to come together to form a community of practice and start to identify possible best practices to improve student outcomes and improve program resilience.
takeaway: Attendees will identify common issues and discuss possible solutions in the creation and teaching of Game Development in youth educational settings such as High Schools, Middle Schools, Elementary Schools, or youth activity camps.
intended audience: This roundtable is for teachers, administrators and other educators who teach or want to teach Game Development in a Youth Education setting or anyone interested in starting a Game Development Program in their school or school district.
speakers: Brian Bautista(Roseville Joint Union High School District)

session title: Page to Booth: Table-Reading and Performance Recording Live Demo
description: One of the biggest challenges in development is knowing when your game's script is ready for that final recording and implementation in the game. A scene can look great on the page but fall flat once you hear it from your actor's mouths – what then? Too often script issues are surfaced late in the process, when fixes would be rushed, prohibitively expensive, or creatively compromised. This session proposes a practical, hands-on solution: teaching teams how to 'beta test' their scripts collaboratively, utilizing the entire pipeline of writers, actors, directors and developers. Complete with a live script-reading demonstration by renowned game actors, this original session demystifies the performance pipeline, strengthens cross-disciplinary trust, and equips developers with a practical method to ensure that what looks good on paper also sounds great out loud.
takeaway: Attendees will leave with a framework for running internal table reads; strategies for preparing scripts for voice and performance capture and identifying issues early; how to work with actors, directors and audio technicians; and a better sense of the pressures connected to shipping compelling narrative content.
intended audience: This session is for all developers at all levels, but will be especially helpful for writers, performance and VO directors and producers, actors, and those involved with audio.
speakers: Alexa Ray Corriea(Gearbox), Sam Maggs(Critical Role), Ben Starr(Independent), Jennifer Hale(Independent), Khris Brown(Vox Point LLC), Amanda Rose Smith(Independent)

session title: Production Workshop Part 2: Alpha to Launch
description:
This year's Production Workshop examines every stage of the game development lifecycle, from Conception to Launch, highlighting the critical hard and soft skills required to successfully navigate them.

This session is a deep dive into solving complex, real-world problems. Far from a 'Production 101' course, this is a fully interactive day featuring roundtable exercises and collaborative discussions. Beyond the planned curriculum, participants are encouraged to workshop their own past and current challenges with the help of facilitators and peers.

The afternoon also features a panel of experienced producers representing a wide range of expertise, from Casual and Mobile games to AAA, Co-Dev partnerships, and Publishing. Following the panel, the day concludes with a 'Quick Fire' mentoring session where attendees will have the opportunity to sit down with the speakers for direct insights and advice.



**Hosts:** Brynley Gibson (Independent), Dave Ranyard (Dream Reality Interactive), and Fleur Marty (Gearbox).

**Morning Session Schedule**




· 10:30am – 10:45am: Welcome and Introduction




· 10:45am – 12:20pm: Workshop Part 1: Conception to Alpha An interactive workshop identifying common development challenges during conception, pre-production, and early production, and the critical skills producers need to navigate them.




· 12:20pm – 1:50pm: Lunch Break




**Afternoon Session Schedule**




· 1:50pm – 3:30pm: Workshop Part 2: Alpha to Launch An interactive workshop focusing on the challenges faced during post-production and closing, emphasizing the specific producer toolkits required to cross the finish line.




· 3:30pm – 3:45pm: Coffee Break




· 3:45pm – 4:30pm: Panel: The Many Roles of the Producer (Moderated by Fleur Marty; Panelists: Daphné Moyal (Virtuos), Ella Vance (The New York Times), Krystal Cooper (Rochester Institute of Technology), Shelby Martin (2K) and Zachary Beaudoin (T-Minus Zero Entertainment)) Experts from across the industry, covering everything from Indie to AAA, share their perspectives on the versatile and evolving nature of the Producer role.




· 4:30pm – 5:30pm: Quick Fire Mentoring (Brynley Gibson, Dave Ranyard, Fleur Marty, and panelists) Experienced producers offer one-on-one guidance and rapid feedback on your specific production challenges.
takeaway: Attendees will learn actionable strategies for tackling complex development problems. You will leave with new tools for your production kit and expert advice on your specific workplace hurdles.
intended audience: This workshop is designed for producers at all levels who are looking for practical tools to solve tough problems.
speakers: Fleur Marty(Gearbox Montreal), Dave Ranyard(Dream Reality Interactive), Brynley Gibson(Independent), Ella Vance(The New York Times), Daphné Moyal(Virtuos Montréal), Zachary Beaudoin(T-Minus Zero Entertainment), Shelby Martin(2K), Pamina Bou(Dream Reality Interactive), Krystal Cooper(Rochester Institute of Technology)

session title: Refreshing the Core Mechanics Fun After 1 Year of LiveOps in 'Infinity Nikki'
description: Infinity Nikki showcases how a large live-ops game can successfully refresh core mechanics after launch through disciplined iteration and modular redesign. This session details the evolution of traversal, combat, and progression systems over the first year, revealing how thoughtful updates improved player engagement, balanced monetization, and extended retention across platforms.
takeaway: Attendees will learn practical strategies for iterating foundational gameplay in live-ops games, including refining abilities, modularizing progression, and aligning design with player goals. This session offers insights on sustaining fun and monetization post-launch, demonstrating how continuous evolution keeps large-scale games vibrant and engaging.
intended audience: No prior experience necessary. Anyone interested in live-ops is welcome to attend!

session title: Shaders 501: Compute for Tech Artists
description:
The features and ease of use of game engines have grown alongside improvements in graphics hardware capabilities. Previously challenging and advanced techniques reserved only for graphics programmers to submit as white papers, the technology is now available to clever technical artists who are willing to put in a bit of elbow grease.

Feel free bring a laptop to 'sketch' along in your own projects!

Epic Games' Matt Oztalay will show you just how easy it is to harness the power of compute shaders to build ever more advanced, and gameplay-relevant, graphics features for your next project.
takeaway: Techniques for implementing compute-like shaders and custom distance fields in game engines, and example uses for those techniques.
intended audience: Tech Artists looking to expand their capabilities for building more advanced graphics features.

session title: State of Level Design 2026
description: A moderator-led discussion with industry thought-leaders covering the current state of level design, advances we've seen in the past year, and the challenges we face in the near future.
takeaway: A understanding of how level design is evolving with the industry and new development practices, presented not just from a single viewpoint, but capture the breadth of real-world experience provided by our GDC speakers all in a single panel.
intended audience: Anyone interested in the art or production of levels and content for modern games.
speakers: Seth Marinello(Double Fine), Cameron Williams(Absurd Ventures), Dan Qiao(Obsidian Entertainment), Miles Tost(CD Projekt RED), Scott Warner(Natural Motion Games)

session title: Tech Leadership Roundtable: Engineering and Strategy
description:
Leading the technology of a project can be a daunting task and it can often feel like a trek into uncharted territory. This roundtable invites hard-won lessons, candid insights, and practical advice between engineering leaders who share the challenge of managing the big picture of technology across games and teams.


In this session, attendees will propose and vote on discussion topics. Expected themes include: architectural design, long-term planning, collaboration between departments, balancing performance and stability with the delivery of the team, and more. We'll move through selected topics in open conversation, guided by experienced moderators.
Bring your problems and questions. Be ready to share solutions and insight. If you've wondered how to tackle the big picture of tech — you're not alone.
takeaway: Attendees will leave with real-world insights into managing technology across a team and learn from the experiences of their peers. They will gain perspective of the most important challenges that they and their peers are facing, as well as ideas that they can take back to their teams and put into practice.
intended audience: This roundtable is targeted for engineering directors and leads who are responsible for the technology strategy for games, teams and studios. The topics will be focused on high level challenges, but no formal leadership title is required and the roundtable will additionally be of interest to team members who would like to better understand those challenges, and also those who aspire to take those challenges on in the future.
speakers: Colin Walder(CD PROJEKT RED), Charles Tremblay(CD PROJEKT RED)

session title: The Modular and Expressive World Art of 'Keeper'
description: The Modular and Expressive World Art of Keeper will be a detailed run-through of the environment art development pipeline for the game. It will go over using Adobe Modeler (VR) for game environment art, the teams proprietary tech for asset instance variation, their painterly foliage brush stroke application, and their mesh morphing tech.
takeaway: Attendees will learn how the Keeper team was able to hide the repetition of assets in their environmental art while maintaining an efficient workflow, ways to approach expressive asset creation for games with VR, and methods for transitioning arbitrary meshes into each other.
intended audience:
This talk will be palatable for a general audience but artists will find the most interest.
There are no explicit prerequisites required to understand this lecture, but basic familiarity with world building, model sculpting and working with Unreal Engine may be helpful.

session title: Understanding AI in Game Audio Roundtable (Presented by the IGDA)
description: In the roundtable for Understanding AI in Game Audio, the AI Working Group co-chairs will present findings and best practices for AI not only in the generative space but also in tooling. We will present the WIKI for roundtable members and encourage frank but respectful discussion in this space and answer any questions regarding AI in Game Audio, with a code of conduct and ethics briefly outlined at the start of the talk. This talk is hosted by the IGDA IA-SIG.
takeaway: All participants should feel informed as to the direction AI in game audio is taking, with tools introduced, approach to the iterative process in game development, and the usage of those tools and best practices. AI is another layer along with sampling and synthesis that needs to be factored into the creative process. Protection including negotiating skills and understanding legal rights is critical, though the SIG is not in any way qualified to give legal advice, it will provide links and resources to consult to do so. There is also great potential to increase creative power in the AI space, and attendees will have knowledge to enable this potential.
intended audience: This roundtable is intended to inform and empower all those in the interactive audio space. Understanding AI in Game Audio is to present progress on a comprehensive and ethical framework of recommended best practices for the development, deployment, and use of audio AI tools. The AI Working Group as part of the IA-SIG and IGDA are committed to fostering transparency, fairness, and accountability in the rapidly evolving field of audio AI by collaborating with stakeholders across technology, academia, industry, and creatives, and this roundtable will provide valuable information and discussion to audio professionals, students and independents in these spaces to navigate this field.
speakers: Alexander Brandon(Funky Rustic, LLC), Pat Scandalis(moForte Inc)

session title: Agentic LiveOps: From Concept to Production (Presented by Amazon Web Services)
description: Agentic systems are creating new opportunities in LiveOps, from player segmentation to automated game operations. Amazon Bedrock AgentCore provides the foundation teams need to move from prototype to production with confidence. In this session, we demonstrate how AgentCore simplifies multi-agent orchestration, provides built-in security controls, enables comprehensive monitoring, and supports robust evaluation frameworks. Using real-world LiveOps examples, you'll see how teams can build scalable, production-grade agents while maintaining the reliability and observability that LiveOps demands. Whether you're building your first agent or scaling existing automation, you'll leave with a practical framework for deploying agentic AI in production gaming environments.
takeaway: Attendees will understand the critical gap between agent prototypes and production systems, learn how Amazon Bedrock AgentCore solves orchestration, security, monitoring, and evaluation challenges, and gain a practical framework for rapidly deploying production-ready agentic LiveOps solutions that scale reliably.
intended audience: This session is suitable for LiveOps engineers, product managers, technical directors, and developers interested in production AI agent deployment. Basic familiarity with LiveOps concepts and AI/ML fundamentals helpful but not required. We'll focus on bridging strategy and implementation for scalable automation.

session title: Community Clubhouse Open Networking Session (Presented by Community Clubhouse)
description: The games industry thrives when people across disciplines, career stages, and perspectives connect directly. This open networking session brings together developers, studio leaders, Trust & Safety professionals, students, job seekers, investors, and operators in one shared space to build relationships that move the industry forward.
takeaway:
Attendees will leave with meaningful new connections, visibility into what others are building, and opportunities for collaboration, hiring, mentorship, or partnership.

There are no panels or presentations. The value is in the room. Come ready to introduce yourself, share what you're working on or looking for, and meet people outside your usual circle.
intended audience:
• Studio leaders and operators
• Indie developers and founders
• Publishers and investors
• Trust & Safety and policy professionals
• AI and live service teams
• Students pursuing careers in games
• Early-career professionals
• Individuals actively seeking new roles
• Anyone building, supporting, or exploring the future of games

session title: Freedom Through Structure: Character Pipelines at Embark (Presented by Houdini)
description: In this session Erik and Björn take you through the development journey of Embark Studios character pipeline. They discuss leveraging Houdini and USD as the core of their workflows. How it allows them the flexibility of having technical and art focused team members to work in the same shared space. The presenters explore having a procedural mentality, showing concrete examples of working within a range from manual to automated. Using USD in combination with Houdini's PDG and Solaris contexts in order to maximize artistic freedom while minimizing repetitive work. Enabling Embark to support their games Arc Raiders and The Finals, each with varying needs, continuously exploring new solutions in live production.
takeaway: Attendees will gain insight into how Embark works with characters. Why using Houdini allows them to balance automation with artistic freedom. And what a node-based workflow enables when working on multiple projects.
intended audience: For artists and technical people alike, that are curious about how USD and Houdini can evolve their workflows.
speakers: Erik Östsjö(Embark Studios), Björn Arvidsson(Embark Studios)

session title: Is Your LiveOps on Life Support? How to Bring It Back to Life (Presented by Community Clubhouse)
description:
For game studios, live game operations often are major revenue drivers, especially with the rising costs of new user acquisition. LiveOps is a critical way to reduce churn, giving players new gameplay experiences worth sticking around for, rather than attempting to engage and monetize new players.

As the CMO and Head of Community of Pixels, a social gaming platform blending farming, exploration, and community building in an open-world universe, Heidi Christine has managed 80+ LiveOps events in two years. She'll break down the five pillars of effective LiveOps, share the planning systems used to move fast, and highlight three case studies where creative LiveOps execution led to real, measurable results.
takeaway: Attendees will gain a 3-tier LiveOps framework, a system for filling content gaps, and tested community collaboration methods to reduce churn. Learn to craft LiveOps strategies that boost retention using no-stakes events, cultural moments, and tools for rapid content deployment.
intended audience: Community managers, game designers, and LiveOps professionals will benefit from this session. The intended audience is those seeking to enhance player retention through strategic and creative LiveOps. Basic knowledge of community management or LiveOps is recommended to grasp concepts like content gaps, no-stakes events, and tooling for rapid content deployment.
speakers: Kalie Moore(High Vibe PR), Heidi Christine(Pixels)

session title: Performance Tuning for Unreal Engine Mobile Development (Presented by Arm)
description: Developing for mobile means battling finite resources. This talk presents a playbook for achieving maximum performance with your Unreal Engine mobile game. We'll analyze how to efficiently manage CPU scheduling, GPU draw calls, and memory footprints. Learn profiling tools and specific tips needed to implement optimizations that radically improve both loading speed and real-time mobile gameplay.

session title: Proving AI Can Produce Results: Helpshift's Evolution into Player Engagement (Presented by Helpshift a Keywords Studios Platform)
description:
We know that player disengagement erodes the outcomes you value the most:
Player retention and satisfaction
LTV
VIPs and monetization
Community health and safety
In this session, we'll go deep into how you can unlock new opportunities to improve the outcomes you care about, by shifting the perspective on player support into player engagement.
Join Rakesh Mistry (Product and GTM Director, Keywords Studios) and Erik Ashby (Head of R&D, Helpshift) for a masterclass exploring the causal factors that drive player disengagement and an exclusive showcase of Care AI, the industry's first truly player-centric AI agent - including results of recent beta trials that will surprise you.
takeaway: Learn how leading studios are rethinking player support as a proactive engagement layer. You'll leave with practical frameworks for identifying disengagement earlier, applying AI responsibly within player care, and integrating support more effectively into live-ops and community strategy.
intended audience: This talk targets all studio leaders, heads of player experience, player support, VIP management, and anyone in the industry who is interested in how AI can influence and improve the player experience.
speakers: Rakesh Mistry(Helpshift, A Keywords Studios Platform), Erik Ashby(Helpshift, A Keywords Studios Platform)

session title: Snapdragon, 'Honor of Kings', and Tencent GVoice: On-Device AI ASR Immersive In-Game Voice Experiences (Presented by Qualcomm Technologies, Inc.)
description:
Snapdragon has partnered with 'Honor of Kings' and Tencent's GVoice to enable on-device deployment of an AI automatic speech recognition (ASR) model for gaming.

Powered by the strong computing power of the Qualcomm® Hexagon™ NPU, the solution delivers real-time speech-to-text (STT) even in complex in-game scenarios, enabling low latency and, high recognition accuracy, and improved power efficiency, for smoother player communication.

This collaboration demonstrates the deep integration between the Snapdragon platform and GVoice in on-device AI speech technology, bringing players a more immersive gaming experience while laying the groundwork for real-time interaction between humans and AI.
takeaway: On-device AI speech in gaming: real-time, low-latency, and production-ready through Snapdragon × GVoice collaboration.
intended audience: Game Developers, Engineers, Technical Designers, and Gameplay programmers interested in integrating scalable AI powered systems that enhance player experiences.
speakers: Lily Zhu(Tencent), Jane Tian(Qualcomm Technologies, Inc.)

session title: The Data Driven Advantage: Optimizing VR Games with Analytics (Presented by Meta)
description: Discover how to transform your data in the Meta Horizon Developer Dashboard into a powerful growth engine for your VR titles. Featuring case studies from AEXLAB (VAIL VR) and Caveman Game Studio (Contractors), we analyze the specific metrics that drove success for these games. Learn how to identify player friction points, optimize store conversion, and leverage platform level insights to refine your development roadmap. This session provides a practical framework for data driven decision making, whether you are scaling a live service hit or launching your first game without custom telemetry.

session title: The Hidden Ones: Real-Time AI Generation of Kung Fu Motions (Presented by Tencent Games AI)
description: The Hidden Ones, a 3D fighting game by Tencent Games, showcases the beauty and precision of Kung Fu through lifelike character motion. Traditional motion generation methods often suffer from visual artefacts such as foot sliding and rigid transitions. Previous AI solutions failed to meet the demands of real-time applications due to limitations in model size, inference speed, and post-processing complexity. This talk introduces a novel real-time AI technique for generating fluid Kung Fu motion transitions, designed specifically for the demands of fighting games. The solution combines marker-less motion capture for data collection, a dedicated generation model, and streamlined post-processing for deployment. Compared to conventional approaches, it significantly reduces data acquisition costs, shrinks package size, speeds up inference, and generates smoother, more natural transitions—especially in nuanced movements like wrists and feet. Already implemented in The Hidden Ones, this technique elevates visual realism and Kung Fu motion fluidity in fast-paced, real-time combat scenarios.
takeaway: Attendees will learn about the challenges of applying real-time Fung Fu motion generation in 3D fighting games and a novel solution to apply game animation AI to reduce resource consumption while ensuring motion quality.
intended audience: This lecture is for AI developers and those interested in Gen AI and animation system including animation generation. Having basic knowledge of game animation system and knowledge about ai application would be helpful but is not required. All interested attendees are welcome.

session title: Triple Play for Revenue Growth: Web Stores + Linkouts + Partners (Presented by Coda)
description: Most publishers treat web stores as a one-off project. The real upside comes when you design the full system. In this session, we share a simple model for out-of-app growth built around three levers: a web store that converts, linkouts that do not leak users, and the partners you need to really scale globally. We will break down what works, what usually goes wrong, and what to prioritise first if you want measurable incremental revenue. Clear examples, real lessons, and takeaways you can use straight away.
takeaway: Introduce the 'triple play' framework and show how web stores, link-outs, and the right strategic partners can combine to drive meaningful and sustained incremental revenue. Learn what it takes to make and run a successful web store, how to optimize link-outs and how to scale globally through payments, voucherisation, and distribution partnerships to unlock net-new demand.
intended audience: This session targets game publishers, commercial leaders, product managers, and growth teams responsible for distribution and monetization strategy. It benefits teams navigating regulatory change and alternative channels. No deep legal background is required; a general understanding of game publishing, platform economics, and digital monetization is sufficient.
speakers: Zac Liew(Coda), Daria Berdnikova(Coda), Junichi Sakishima(Digital Garage, Inc.)

session title: Unpub Playtesting
description:
Playing new games is fun, but helping create them is even better! Play the tabletop games of the future today in our playtesting area hosted by Unpub. There's no registration required for players — seats are first-come first-served.

If you are a designer looking to have your game playtested, you must have reserved a table in advance. Reservations are now closed. More info: https://www.unpub.org/gdc (https://www.unpub.org/gdc)
location: Tabletop Free Play & Playtesting Lounge, West Hall Level 3
speakers: No speakers found for this session

session title: Augmenting NPCs with LLMs: Practical Experience from NC State University & Arm Developer Labs (Presented by Arm)
description:
Non‑player characters (NPCs) are moving beyond pre‑scripted trees to responsive agents that converse, plan, and act in context. In this talk we share practical lessons from an Arm Developer Labs project run in partnership with North Carolina State University (NCSU), where a team of Master's students is building a proof‑of‑concept, puzzle‑style game that showcases LLM‑augmented NPCs running on Arm‑based devices.

We walk through the end‑to‑end approach the student team uses to keep gameplay smooth while adding intelligence: selecting and sizing models for on‑device inference, applying quantization to meet 30–60 FPS budgets, and using structured generation so model outputs map deterministically to in‑engine actions.
takeaway:
Game Design Insights: See how AI‑driven NPCs can enhance puzzle gameplay and player engagement.

Lessons Learned: Honest review of challenges, trade‑offs, and best practices from the student project.

session title: Automatic Wrapping of Facial Expression Scans with WrapAI (Presented by Faceform)
description: In this talk, we'll present a pipeline for fully automated wrapping and cleanup of dozens of FACS scans using Faceform WrapAI. Building a likeness rig from real FACS scans often involves a lot of manual work. We'll demonstrate how this can be solved in just a few clicks, with a level of quality suitable for silver-level likeness characters.
takeaway:
Starting with a set of scanned expressions, we'll show how a chain of WrapAI projects can automatically:
- wrap all expressions to a common template,
- stabilize the head,
- fix eyelid closures and lip intersections,
- decompose scanned expressions into clean, isolated, rig-ready blendshapes.
You'll see the key processing stages and the reasoning behind each step.
intended audience: This talk is useful for character artists, scanning technicians, and riggers — anyone working with facial scan data. It's also relevant for artists building custom MetaHuman likeness rigs.

session title: Discovery Decoded: Your Guide to the Meta Horizon Store (Presented by Meta)
description: Gain an inside look at how the Meta Horizon Store surfaces content to players across its entire ecosystem. This session demystifies the complex interplay of ranking signals, metadata optimization, and manual promotional mechanics. Hear directly from Mahncy Marotra, the Product Lead who helped build these systems, to learn how to improve your app's visibility through data-driven, strategic optimization. Attendees will walk away with a systematic toolkit of actions to optimize their store presence and drive sustainable growth.

session title: Games as the New Social Arenas: Building Communities That Self-Regulate and Thrive (Presented by Community Clubhouse)
description:
Games have become the modern world's social commons, where friendships form, conflicts
play out, and culture is created in real time. This panel brings together community designers,
trust & safety leads, and game directors to explore practical strategies for how studios can
nurture positive community behavior and transform toxicity into teachable moments.

Panelists will share:
 Moderation frameworks that scale from indie to AAA
 Design interventions that reward pro-social behavior without feeling heavy-handed
 Player education systems that shift mindsets rather than simply punishing actions
 Case studies from studios that transformed repeat offenders into positive community
leaders through targeted intervention

Through data-driven insights and lived experience, the panel will demonstrate how empathy-
driven design, transparent communication, and systemic thinking can build resilient
communities that self-regulate and thrive.
takeaway:
Attendees will leave with actionable frameworks for designing games and moderation systems
that cultivate positive player behavior at scale.
intended audience:
Community managers, game designers, producers, trust & safety professionals, and studio
leadership invested in sustainable community health.
speakers: Hege Tokerud(Aiba), Karin Johnson(Magic Potion Games), Christina Camilleri(Netflix Games), Colin Cragg(Blue Scarab Entertainment)

session title: How Emerging Hardware Innovation Will Change the Player Experience
description:
Join Bill Kleyman (Co-founder and CEO of Apolo.us) and Robert Shearer (Corporate Vice President, AMD) to understand the transformative impact of AI and hardware innovation on gaming and beyond. This session will delve into the evolution of AI from its early days of rack building to its current role in cloud-based production environments, highlighting the challenges of scaling AI for millions of players. Discover how emerging technologies like GPU acceleration, edge computing, and next-gen microprocessors are reshaping user experiences and game development.

The discussion will also address the integration of AI into gaming consoles, the future of local AI processing, and the broader implications of hardware advancements for industries like autonomous vehicles. Gain insights into data center & hardware leaders are driving innovation to meet the demands of the next decade. Don't miss this thought-provoking conversation on the future of gaming and AI.
location: Blue Shield of California Theater, YBCA

session title: Neural Super Resolution and Frame Generation for Snapdragon Mobile Devices (Presented by Qualcomm Technologies, Inc.)
description: In this talk, the audience will learn about Qualcomm Technologies' upcoming AI-based frame upscaling technology. We will go over some of the benefits of using this solution, how to integrate it into a game engine, and some of the results you can expect by using AI Frame Fusion.
takeaway: The audience will learn about the upcoming AI Frame Fusion technology and how it might be beneficial to them to integrate in existing and upcoming mobile projects.
intended audience: This talk is intended for those looking to know more about the latest technologies for the Snapdragon Mobile Platform and are looking to increase graphical fidelity while lowering the amount of on-device power required.
speakers: Kevin Hawkins(Qualcomm Technologies, Inc.), Rob VanReenen(Qualcomm Technologies, Inc.), Matthew Hoffman(Qualcomm Technologies, Inc.)

session title: Real-Time Speech AI for Games with Tencent: Squad Comms, AI Teammates, and Esports Commentary (Presented by Amazon Web Services)
description: Speech AI for games must work fast, integrate seamlessly, and perform under demanding conditions: noisy in-game comms, emotionally charged callouts, and rapid meta shifts. This session will cover an end-to-end approach to training and shipping speech-driven systems for games—real-time comms moderation and coaching, a multilingual AI teammate that understands and speaks naturally, and simultaneous translation for e-sports commentary. The talk will explore how Tencent balanced latency, cost, safety, and live-ops iteration using AWS as the infrastructure layer.
takeaway: Attendees will learn a practical blueprint for building and shipping speech AI in games: training data strategy, low-latency inference, evaluation for noisy multiplayer audio, safety/moderation, multilingual voice interactions, and live-ops iteration. Includes pitfalls, system patterns, and integration guidance for comms, AI teammates, and live translation.
intended audience: For game AI/ML engineers, audio/voice engineers, backend/platform teams, and producers planning speech features for multiplayer, companions, or broadcast. Best for teams shipping live services. Pre-reqs: basic ML concepts and familiarity with game networking/online latency constraints; no deep speech research required.

session title: Unleash Artistic Creativity: How Tencent's AIGC Tools Empower Creators (Presented by Tencent Games AI)
description: This Session presents how to leverage AIGC capabilities to reduce repetitive manual labor for artists, allowing them to dedicate more energy and time to creative work. The session focuses on best practices for developing AIGC tools within the 3D game character production pipeline, including but not limited to: 3D structural sketch model generation, auto-retopology, and auto-rigging.
takeaway:
1. Best practices for leveraging AIGC tools to reduce repetitive manual labor in the 3D character art pipeline.
2. Practical experience in developing AIGC tools in 3D art pipeline.
intended audience: 3D modler; Rigging artist; Engineer for AI tools.

session title: 15 More Games: Hands-On
description: Bring a friend and enjoy a unique, hands-on gaming session. We'll be playing several of the games that Stone has created for his family over the past 15+ years. Build robot combatants, join in a rhythmic drum circle, destroy enemy castles, design your own monster, and more! Play by the rules, or make up your own. Stone will be there to teach the games, talk about their unique constraints, and answer any questions you may have. There is no formal presentation, so feel free to arrive and leave at any time during this session.
takeaway: Stop by to socialize with fellow game developers while playing one-of-a-kind tabletop board games. After the session, we hope you'll be inspired to create a custom game to play with your closest friends and family members.
intended audience: Open to all!

session title: AI Psychosis Roundtable: Reclaiming the Female Voice in an Algorithmic World (Presented by the IGDA)
description:
As AI transforms creative workflows and studio cultures, many women and female-identifying professionals in game development are facing new challenges and new forms of invisibility. Generative tools promise efficiency, but they also risk erasing nuance, authorship, and the human touch that has been hard won through decades of advocacy for inclusion and recognition.
In this roundtable, we'll unpack the idea of AI Psychosis, a metaphor for the collective disorientation that can occur when studios prioritize machine output over human insight. We'll discuss how this shift uniquely impacts women in creative, technical, and leadership roles. From loss of visibility in algorithm driven pipelines to emotional burnout from navigating AI accelerated expectations.
Hosted by the Women in Games SIG+, this session invites open dialogue about reclaiming creative identity, protecting equity gains, and leading with empathy in an AI-saturated landscape. Participants will share stories, strategies, and solutions for staying grounded, visible, and valued in the next evolution of game development.
takeaway: Participants will leave with a clearer understanding of how to recognize and recover from 'AI Psychosis' within creative teams where automation has begun to overshadow artistry. Attendees will gain actionable strategies for leading with empathy, re-centering human creativity, and fostering psychologically safe environments that embrace AI as a tool, not a master.
intended audience: This roundtable is designed for women, nonbinary, and gender-diverse professionals working across all areas of game development, as well as allies seeking to understand and support them in an AI-transformed industry. It's especially relevant for creatives, producers, and leaders navigating how to protect human creativity, ethical authorship, and emotional wellbeing in a world increasingly defined by algorithms.
speakers: Dana Ware(Hidden Realities/The VOID), Audrey Perera(Zero Space)

session title: Animation Roundtable Day 1
description:
The Animation Roundtable aims to bring all disciplines together under a common cause- how to best implement and improve the field of animation in game development. The following questions are among those that will be addressed:

Who should be directing the animation in a game? Who owns each part of the animation pipeline after export? How can all disciplines work together better, to bring the best looking and feeling animation to their game? What workflow frustrations have been experienced and how were they resolved? What new technologies are available or coming online that can help animators excel at their jobs, and what challenges will those technologies bring? What is your best tip or trick that you think everyone needs to hear? What mistakes have you made that you think others can learn from? How can we all give and take feedback better, from all disciplines?

In addition, this roundtable discussion will focus on helping newcomers by dedicating the last part of each session to them, encouraging questions to help them get prepared (or jump-started!) in their animation careers.
takeaway: The roundtable participants will walk away with a better understanding of how to best communicate with their coworkers in order to deliver, as a team, the best animation in their games as possible. Participants may also pick up technical tips and tricks, as well as career advice for getting started in games or career advancement.
intended audience: Audience could include animators, designers, technical animators, producers, and programmers who are excited to participate in a productive discussion about animation and team building in games.
speakers: Euna Park(That's No Moon), Shayna Moon(CD Projekt RED)

session title: Being an Effective Ally Roundtable (Presented by the IGDA)
description: In Being an Effective Ally we discuss simple, everyday ways to support marginalized groups in their workplaces and communities. Participants learn techniques that work at the office, social events, conferences, and online. Attendees are encouraged to bring up personal experiences and hypothetical scenarios for group discussion. Being an Effective Ally is hosted by the IGDA Allies SIG.
takeaway: At the end of the Being an Effective Ally session, participants will feel more confident in speaking up to support marginalized groups and be more aware of the challenges facing them in their workplaces and communities.
intended audience: Being an Effective Ally is intended for everyone interested in improving their skills in speaking up for and supporting marginalized groups. Anyone can be an ally and this discussion allows participants to explore how to handle uncomfortable situations in the real world.

session title: Creating Player Expertise Microtalks
description:
Video games have become a mainstream entertainment medium, and with that comes an increasing diversity in gamers' gaming backgrounds. We are moving on from the previous debate of 'what are games' to the reality of game devs needing to tailor their designs to players who are coming into the game spaces from different generations and levels of genre expertise.


In this series of microtalks, developers from various backgrounds will talk about lessons they've learned from their experiences matching their designs to potential players' experience and expectations, and the tools that have helped them onboard players for the maximum fun gameplay experience.
takeaway: Audiences should come out of this talk with tools and tips on understanding target audiences' gaming background, preferences, and expertise. How to make informed decisions in design, development, and beyond to avoid target mismatch and maximize success.
intended audience: This talk would benefit people who work in game development for general audiences across different backgrounds, or people who work in education/training/other games with a purpose that targets non-traditional gamers. As well as anyone interested in improving the inclusivity of their games.
speakers: Monica Fan(Pipework Studio), Ian Schreiber(Independent), John Ryan(Independent), Michael Jones(Independent), Lauryn Ash(Stealth Startup)

session title: Designing the Final Level of 'Split Fiction'
description:
Split Fiction is Hazelight's third co-op exclusive game, preceded by A Way Out and It Takes Two. This talk explores how the gimmick of Split Fiction's final level turned out to be the largest co-op design challenge so far, and why its unique concept was saved for the end of the game.

Designer Hannes Gille dives into the obstacles, and respective solutions that were discovered when designing this dual-world level. Different techniques were used to promote communication, collaboration and to create jaw dropping moments. The presenter walks through the design strategy used to reveal the concept, how the ingredients to make satisfying co-op puzzles were discovered, and why simplifying the design can give the players more room to be blown away.

Warning for spoilers!
takeaway: Attendees will walk away from this session with a deeper understanding of the design challenges with creating a co-op game. They will also be given tools to encourage cooperative communication between players.
intended audience: Anyone curious about the design process of Split Fiction, in particular developers interested in co-op games. Since the presenter goes through parts of the ending of Split Fiction, attendees who want to fully avoid spoilers should have reached the end of the game.

session title: External Development (Outsourcing) Roundtable Day 1
description: External Development, or Outsourcing as it is commonly referred, plays a huge role in the game development industry and with it comes a huge amount of challenges. The External Development Roundtable is a moderated forum discussion surrounding external development topics which are mainly generated by the participants. Whether it's team/leadership buy-in, career growth, best practices, vendor relations, etc. this roundtable will provide a forum for questions, ideas and solutions from industry peers on the front lines of external development. Attendees from all experience levels and disciplines are welcome and will benefit from the discussion. Attendees are strongly encouraged to participate in the conversation.
takeaway: Attendees should leave with an increased awareness of the challenges and solutions that come with external development and how different studios, developers and service providers approach these challenges. They will have a better understanding of external development which they can apply to their own pipeline for more successful external engagements.
intended audience: The primary audience for this session are game developers and producers, outsource/ExDev managers and studio team leadership across all disciplines as well as service providers who are interested in candidly discussing topics related to the challenges and successes of Outsourcing and External Partnerships in game development.

session title: Games About Games Microtalks
description: 'Games about Games' are a rising trend in interactive works which are self reflexively about video games history, culture, and development. They often feature developers, players, or the history of the medium itself. Some works are nonfiction, others invent personnas or consoles, with many blurring the line between the two. This panel seeks to discuss and dissect this rising trend in game development, with microtalks from across the industry that expand on the logistical and narrative challenges of making games that are about games.
takeaway: In a medium that is often derivative of older works, hearing a variety of perspectives surrounding game self-reflexivity can allow attendees to find new ways to think about the development and the processes of our industry and its history. This can also, in turn, inform future game creation practices.
intended audience: This series of micro-talks is suited for game designers and artists looking for experimentation taking place within the realm of independent game development as well as writers and educators who hope to examine the trend of these self-reflexive games.
speakers: Nick Lives(Night Signal Entertainment LLC), Jon Perry(Quibble Media, LLC), Adam Pype(BEESWAX GAMES), Lucas Immanuel(Glootschke Games, LLC), Jonny Hopkins(boshi's place), Flan Falacci(Flan Falacci), Juan Hernandez(Recurring Dream), Gurn Group(boshi's place), mut moochi(boshi's place)

session title: How We Draw a 3D Sprite World: The Stylized Art of 'Never's End'
description: The team at Hypersect wanted to stand out from the competition with the art style of its tactical RPG, Never's End, by solving the problem of rendering a true 3D world as if it was hand-drawn 2D pixel art. In this session, Ryan Juckett (game director and lead engineer) will share details about the game's art pipeline and rendering technology powered by Hypersect's proprietary game engine. Characters and the environment are fully 3D modeled allowing a level of fidelity not possible in a true 2D sprite engine. While maintaining a hand-animated sprite illusion, Never's end allows for full camera control, dynamic lighting, complex materials such as liquids, skeletal based animation of dynamic clothing and weapons, and more.
takeaway: Attendees will learn the many challenges of building a 3D renderer that behaves like a convincing 2D sprite engine, and how they were solved, along with the techniques used to build 3D art that can translate into 2D pixel art.
intended audience: This talk targets programmers, technical artists and 3D artists interested in working with stylized rendering pipelines. We will discuss tools and techniques for art asset creation along with technical details of algorithms and rendering.

session title: Influencing Fandom: Making Games That Market Themselves
description:
Marketing doesn't start with making TikToks or creating a Discord. It starts with game design. But viral videos help too.

Emily Pitcher went from working on failed game to a game with 20+ publishers reaching out, participation in many showcases, and much higher wishlists. In this session, she details how she made that transition, specifically designing a game with a market in mind and making viral videos about her game. Emily gives tips on how to use market research to come up with a game idea that players will engage with and how to turn that idea into videos with millions of views. She shares actionable ways to evaluate your game idea, dissects her best and worst videos, and gives ideas for videos you can make about your game.
takeaway: Attendees will leave with actionable tips on how to come up with more engaging game ideas based off market research and how to make better videos about their game.
intended audience: The intended audience of this talk is indie game developers who are serious about making profitable games and want to grow a community for their game. Attendees do not need prior experience making social media content to understand this talk.

session title: Juniors, Associates, Interns: Why They Matter and How to Support Them
description: As the industry bleeds talent from mass layoffs, it's more crucial than ever to not only bring juniors onto teams, but also set them up for success so they can thrive and begin the path towards becoming seniors. In this panel, industry professionals Ashley Poprik, Emma Kidwell, Sarah Helen Slovak, Natalie Checo, and Samantha Low will inform seniors of the biggest problems that juniors face in the industry. Why is consistent and accurate titling so important? When are you ready to mentor a junior? Why is hiring marginalized talent vital to the health of the industry?
